summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a scorching 12‑7‑71 Felt Forum show with a smoking Smokestack Lightning and gorgeous Brokedown Palace.
review: The recording is a top‑tier soundboard tape – crisp, balanced and loud enough to feel the room, with only occasional right‑channel crackle. Musically the band is in peak form: Jerry’s guitar shines, Pigpen’s harp returns, Keith adds lush keys, and the set rolls tight from the fiery opening into an unforgettable second half highlighted by Smokestack Lightning and Brokedown Palace.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance (70 % weight) and recording quality (30 % weight), with only minor complaints about a brief mud start or channel noise.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crystal‑clear mix, well‑balanced instruments, very loud and full. A few reviewers note a muddy intro and occasional right‑channel crackle, but these are brief and do not detract from overall fidelity.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, high‑energy performance that builds from a fiery first set into an even tighter second set. Dual‑keyboard texture with Pigpen and Keith adds extra color; the band stays in the pocket throughout, delivering classic ’71 material with a raw yet precise feel.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his soaring tone, loud mix, and an especially gorgeous solo on Brokedown Palace.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for melodic, on‑key bass lines; one reviewer notes his “pounding” bass moment in a later show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s debut alongside Pigpen is celebrated as a treat; the two‑keyboard blend receives specific kudos.
